371b16dd1c22.name: Category
371b16dd1c22.statement:
A category $\mathcal{C}$ consists of:

A collection $\text{Ob}(\mathcal{C})$ of objects.

For each pair of objects $A, B$, a set $\text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, B)$ of morphisms from $A$ to $B$.

For each object $A$, an identity morphism $\text{id}_A \in \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, A)$.

For each triple $A, B, C$, a composition map
$$
\circ : \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(B, C) \times \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, B) \to \text{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(A, C)
$$

subject to:

**Associativity.** $h \circ (g \circ f) = (h \circ g) \circ f$ for all composable $f, g, h$.

**Unit laws.** $f \circ \text{id}_A = f = \text{id}_B \circ f$ for all $f : A \to B$.

11da45b52975.name: Uniqueness of identity
11da45b52975.statement: The identity morphism at each object is unique. If $e \in \text{Hom}(A, A)$ satisfies $f \circ e = f$ for all $f$ with domain $A$ and $e \circ g = g$ for all $g$ with codomain $A$, then $e = \text{id}_A$.
11da45b52975.proof: $e = e \circ \text{id}_A = \text{id}_A$, where the first equality uses the right-unit property of $\text{id}_A$ and the second uses the left-unit property of $e$. $\square$

2ecfa94ea706.name: Set and Grp
2ecfa94ea706.statement: The category $\textbf{Set}$ has sets as objects, functions as morphisms, and function composition as composition. The category $\textbf{Grp}$ has groups as objects and group homomorphisms as morphisms.

ad44f4e58c50.name: Initial object
ad44f4e58c50.statement: An object $I \in \mathcal{C}$ is *initial* if for every object $A$ there exists a unique morphism $I \to A$.

c912e677903c.name: Uniqueness of initial objects
c912e677903c.statement: If $I$ and $I'$ are both initial in $\mathcal{C}$, then they are uniquely isomorphic: there exists a unique isomorphism $I \xrightarrow{\sim} I'$.
c912e677903c.proof: By initiality of $I$ there is a unique $f : I \to I'$; by initiality of $I'$ there is a unique $g : I' \to I$. Then $g \circ f : I \to I$ must equal $\text{id}_I$ by uniqueness, and similarly $f \circ g = \text{id}_{I'}$. $\square$

51cf76b68815.notes: The proof uses the unit laws from the definition of a category.

db3470d0e316.notes: Set and Grp are concrete instances of the definition of a category.

1db2ee786615.notes: Initial object is defined within a category.

ebf4d1204fa5.notes: Uniqueness follows from the definition of initial object.
